candidates.evidence_quote: We expect to open as many as 25 new restaurants in fiscal 2025 across our portfolio of concepts.
We anticipate approximately $190 million to $200 million in capital expenditures to support this level of unit development, as well as required maintenance on our restaurants.
As of September 30, 2025, we had net availability for borrowings of $366.5 million, based on no outstanding debt balance and $33.5 million in standby letters of credit under the Revolver Facility.

Under the Revolver Facility, we are subject to financial covenants, as well as to customary events of default that, if triggered, could result in acceleration of the maturity of the Revolver Facility.

On February 28, 2025, we issued $575.0 million in aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes (“2030 Notes”), which will mature on March 15, 2030, unless earlier repurchased, redeemed or converted.

On February 28, 2025, we used part of the net proceeds from the issuance of the 2030 Notes to repurchase approximately $276.0 million aggregate principal amount of the 2026 Notes in a privately-negotiated transaction for aggregate consideration of $289.8 million, which included a premium of $13.8 million.
Common stock dividends of $39.4 million and $39.8 million were paid in the first nine months of fiscal 2025 and 2024, respectively.
In total, we repurchased 2.6 million shares at a cost of $142.7 million, excluding excise tax, during the first nine months of fiscal 2025.
We delivered another quarter of solid results, with revenue within our guidance range
earnings and profitability finishing above the high end of our expectations
Comparable restaurant sales at The Cheesecake Factory restaurants increased 0.3% year-over-year in the third quarter of fiscal 2025.
North Italia operating information: Comparable restaurant sales vs. prior year (3 )% 2 %
delivering year-over-year improvements in labor productivity, wage management, and hourly staff and manager retention
delivering year-over-year improvements in labor productivity, wage management, and hourly staff and manager retention, supporting healthy margin performance
Food and beverage costs 197,654 21.8 % 195,306 22.6 %

Our operators once again executed exceptionally well, delivering year-over-year improvements in labor productivity, wage management, and hourly staff and manager retention, supporting healthy margin performance.
While the restaurant industry is navigating a softer environment, we remain confident in our ability to manage through it
delivering positive comparable sales results amid a more challenging and competitive environment

Our focus on delivering delicious, memorable dining experiences and ongoing menu innovation continues to resonate with guests, reflected in steady sales trends and positive early results from our latest menu updates.
the Company opened two new FRC restaurants, and two Cheesecake Factory restaurants opened internationally under a licensing agreement in Mexico
The Company recorded a pre-tax net expense of $0.8 million primarily related to Fox Restaurant Concepts (“FRC”) acquisition-related expenses.

notes: No fiscal_kpis in this chunk (KPIs only on chunk_001).
Event 0 = 10-Q part 2/2 (liquidity/capital-allocation MD&A + risk-factor pointer); event 1 = Q3 FY2025 earnings 8-K press release. Both reviewed in full, in order.
Namesake-brand comp sales mapped to company-wide same_store_sales because the brand name equals the company legal name (banned in driver_name); North Italia kept as its own brand-level driver.

Skipped 9 items: bakery/third-party bakery business (About-section boilerplate, no causal claim); total available liquidity (overlaps credit_facility_availability); adverse weather/natural disasters (vague boilerplate); unemployment rates (weak boilerplate); landlord/tenant economic health (vague); pandemics/quarantine restrictions (boilerplate); lease termination income (bundled inside impairment line, not separable); 2026 Notes conversion-rate adjustment (single-instance mechanics); FORTUNE 100 Best Companies mention (non-causal).

Safe-harbor macro factors (consumer_spending, government_shutdown, tariff_policy, foreign_exchange_rates, interest_rates, inflation, minimum_wage, supply_chain_disruption) included as candidates: explicitly named, restaurant-relevant, reusable classes — but sourced from boilerplate risk language, not quarter-specific narrative.
All evidence_quote values verified as verbatim substrings of the source event content.
